title: leucanthemum vulgare subsp. vulgare
intro: Leucanthemum vulgare subsp. vulgare, also known as the common dandelion or oxeye daisy, is a perennial plant species in the sunflower family that is native to Europe, western Asia, and northwestern Africa. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and diuretic properties. The health benefits of Leucanthemum vulgare subsp. vulgare include reducing inflammation, improving digestive health, and supporting the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. The plant can be prepared as an herbal tea, infused oil, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
